revision.text: #redirect[[en:Yachinomi-no-Mikoto]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

{{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 八杵命 | 神祇 = [[国津神]] | 全名 = 八杵命 | 別名 = | 別称 = | 神階 = | 神格 = 開拓の神 | 陵所 = 伝承あり | 父 = [[建御名方神]] | 母 = [[八坂刀売神]] | 配偶者 = | 子 = [[久留須神]]、[[比良夫神]]、[[若那売神]]、[[八立神]]、[[倉稲主神]] | 神社 = [[諏訪大社]]、[[矢木神社]] 等 | 関連氏族 = [[小出氏 (禰宜大夫)|小出氏]] }}
'''八杵命'''（やきねのみこと、やぎのみこと）は、[[長野県]][[諏訪地方]]の民間伝承（[[諏訪信仰]]）の[[神 (神道)|神]]
== 概要 == 史料としては『神氏系図』等に見える神で、[[建御名方神]]の子とされるが、[[御子神]]や子孫以外にこれといった記述はない。[[諏訪大社]]上社の'''五官祝'''（ごかんのほうり）の一つ、'''禰宜大夫'''（ねぎだゆう）を務めた'''[[小出氏 (禰宜大夫)|小出氏]]'''の祖神と伝えられるが、詳細な系図は現存しておらず、その全貌は不明である。
[[下諏訪町]]矢木に鎮座する矢木神社では[[信濃国]]開拓の神とされ、神陵が付近にあると伝わるが、詳細な位置は不明。
== 考証 == [[阿波国]]の[[式内社]]である[[八桙神社]]の祭神'''八桙神'''と同一視する説がある。『特撰神名牒』、『大日本史神祇志』では祭神八桙神はな位置はあっぞkz建御名方神の子または曾孫としており<ref>延川和彦「諏訪氏系図」『修補諏訪氏系図.正編』飯田好太郎、大正10年、18頁。</ref><ref>宝賀寿男『長髄彦の後裔とその奉斎神社』2008年</ref>、『諸系譜』第六冊の「長公系図」には、弟を意味する「イロト（色止・伊呂止）」を名にする'''伊侶止命'''の子に'''八上乃命'''（やかみのみこと）があげられており、これが八桙神にあたると考えられる<ref>宝賀寿男『長髄彦の後裔とその奉斎神社』2008年</ref>。
父祖の関係であるが、[[鈴木真年]]はその著『史略名称訓義』において「大和国[[城上郡]]登美ノ人、長髄モ同所ノ邑名、飛鳥[[事代主神]]ノ子」と記し、これは本荘氏の系譜『本荘家譜』において汙麻斯麻尼足尼命（宇摩志麻遅命）の補註に「母飛鳥大神之女、登美夜毘売」と記されていることからとされる。また[[太田亮]]も「磯城彦は即ち三輪氏の他ならず」と指摘している<ref>宝賀寿男「長髄彦と磯城県主の系譜」『三輪氏 大物主神の祭祀者』青垣出版、2015年</ref>。
[[宝賀寿男]]はその行動や世代関係などから、[[長髄彦]]を事代主神の子ではなくその弟と考え、[[建御名方神]]（[[諏訪氏]]祖）、[[天八現津彦命]]、[[観松彦色止命]]（[[長国造]]、[[都佐国造]]祖）と同一という説を唱えた。加えて顕著な竜蛇信仰、本殿を持たない神社、[[銅鐸]]の分布、長野県に分布する「三輪」の地名と三輪信仰、互いに「ミワ」姓を名乗ることから三輪氏、諏訪氏が同族であるとした。また長髄彦の子孫は二つの系統に別れたと考え、一方を[[出早雄命|伊豆速男命]]（[[片倉辺命]]）に始まる諏訪氏、もう一方を[[八杵命]]（八桙神）に始まる長国造、都佐国造とした<ref>宝賀寿男「長髄彦と磯城県主の系譜」『三輪氏 大物主神の祭祀者』青垣出版、2015年</ref>。
== 系譜 == [[建御名方神]]と[[八坂刀売神]]の子の一柱で、子に[[久留須神]]、[[比良夫神]]、[[若那売神]]、[[八立神]]（八玉神）、[[倉稲主神]]（倉稲魂神）がいる。
== 祀る神社 == * [[諏訪大社]]上社前宮 摂社 若御子社（[[長野県]][[茅野市]]宮川） * 諏訪大社下社春宮 摂社 若宮社（長野県[[諏訪郡]][[下諏訪町]]） * 諏訪大社下社秋宮 摂社 若宮社（長野県諏訪郡下諏訪町） * [[矢木神社]]（長野県諏訪郡下諏訪町）
== 参考文献 == {{Reflist}}
== 脚注 == {{Notelist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[諏訪氏]]

== 祀る神社 == * [[諏訪大社]]上社前宮 摂社 若御子社（[[長野県]][[茅野市]]宮川） * 諏訪大社下社春宮 摂社 若宮社（長野県[[諏訪郡]][[下諏訪町]]） * 諏訪大社下社秋宮 摂社 若宮社（長野県諏訪郡下諏訪町） * [[矢木神社]]（長野県諏訪郡下諏訪町）
== 参考文献 == {{Reflist}}
== 脚注 == {{Notelist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[諏訪氏]]
{{デフォルトソート:やきねのみこと}} [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:諏訪信仰]]

{{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 八杵命 | 神祇 = [[国津神]] | 全名 = 八杵命 | 別名 = | 別称 = | 神階 = | 神格 = 開拓の神 | 陵所 = 伝承あり | 父 = [[建御名方神]] | 母 = [[八坂刀売神]] | 配偶者 = | 子 = [[久留須神]]、[[比良夫神]]、[[若那売神]]、[[八立神]]、[[倉稲主神]] | 神社 = [[諏訪大社]]、[[矢木神社]] 等 | 関連氏族 = [[小出氏 (禰宜大夫)|小出氏]] }}
'''八杵命'''（やきねのみこと、やぎのみこと）は、[[長野県]][[諏訪地方]]の民間伝承（[[諏訪信仰]]）の[[神 (神道)|神]]。
== 概要 == 史料としては『神氏系図』等に見える神で、[[建御名方神]]の子とされるが、[[御子神]]や子孫以外にこれといった記述はない。[[諏訪大社]]上社の'''五官祝'''（ごかんのほうり）の一つ、'''禰宜大夫'''（ねぎだゆう）を務めた'''[[小出氏 (禰宜大夫)|小出氏]]'''の祖神と伝えられるが、詳細な系図は現存しておらず、その全貌は不明である。
[[下諏訪町]]矢木に鎮座する矢木神社では[[信濃国]]開拓の神とされ、神陵が付近にあると伝わるが、詳細な位置は不明。
== 考証 == 八杵命は他の史書には見えず、同神を祀る神社も長野県以外に存在するか不明であるが、[[阿波国]]の[[式内社]]である[[八桙神社]]の祭神'''八桙神'''と同一視する説がある。『特撰神名牒』、『大日本史神祇志』では祭神八桙神は建御名方神の子または曾孫としており<ref>延川和彦「諏訪氏系図」『修補諏訪氏系図.正編』飯田好太郎、大正10年、18頁。</ref><ref>宝賀寿男『長髄彦の後裔とその奉斎神社』2008年</ref>、『諸系譜』第六冊の「長公系図」には、弟を意味する「イロト（色止・伊呂止）」を名にする'''伊侶止命'''の子に'''八上乃命'''（やかみのみこと）があげられており、これが八桙神にあたると考えられる<ref>宝賀寿男『長髄彦の後裔とその奉斎神社』2008年</ref>。
父祖の関係であるが、[[鈴木真年]]はその著『史略名称訓義』において「大和国[[城上郡]]登美ノ人、長髄モ同所ノ邑名、飛鳥[[事代主神]]ノ子」と記し、これは本荘氏の系譜『本荘家譜』において汙麻斯麻尼足尼命（宇摩志麻遅命）の補註に「母飛鳥大神之女、登美夜毘売」と記されていることからとされる。また[[太田亮]]も「磯城彦は即ち三輪氏の他ならず」と指摘している<ref>宝賀寿男「長髄彦と磯城県主の系譜」『三輪氏 大物主神の祭祀者』青垣出版、2015年</ref>。
[[宝賀寿男]]はその行動や世代関係などから、[[長髄彦]]を事代主神の子ではなくその弟と考え、[[建御名方神]]（[[諏訪氏]]祖）、[[天八現津彦命]]、[[観松彦色止命]]（[[長国造]]、[[都佐国造]]祖）と同一という説を唱えた。加えて顕著な龍蛇信仰、本殿を持たない神社、[[銅鐸]]の分布、長野県に分布する「三輪」の地名と三輪信仰、互いに「ミワ」姓を名乗ることから三輪氏、諏訪氏が同族の[[海神族]]であるとした。また長髄彦の子孫は二つの系統に別れたと考え、一方を[[出早雄命|伊豆速男命]]（[[片倉辺命]]）に始まる諏訪氏、もう一方を[[八杵命]]（八桙神）に始まる長国造、都佐国造とした<ref>宝賀寿男「長髄彦と磯城県主の系譜」『三輪氏 大物主神の祭祀者』青垣出版、2015年</ref>。
== 系譜 == [[建御名方神]]と[[八坂刀売神]]の子の一柱で、子に[[久留須神]]、[[比良夫神]]、[[若那売神]]、[[八立神]]（八玉神）、[[倉稲主神]]（[[倉稲魂神]]）がいる。
== 祀る神社 == * [[諏訪大社]]上社前宮 摂社 若御子社（[[長野県]][[茅野市]]宮川） * 諏訪大社下社春宮 摂社 若宮社（長野県[[諏訪郡]][[下諏訪町]]） * 諏訪大社下社秋宮 摂社 若宮社（長野県諏訪郡下諏訪町） * [[矢木神社]]（長野県諏訪郡下諏訪町）
== 参考文献 == {{Reflist}}
== 脚注 == {{Notelist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[諏訪氏]]

[[下諏訪町]]矢木に鎮座する矢木神社では[[信濃国]]開拓の神とされ、神陵が付近にあると伝わるが、詳細な位置は不明。
== 考証 == 八杵命は他の史書には見えず、同神を祀る神社も長野県以外に存在するか不明であるが、[[阿波国]]の[[式内社]]である[[八桙神社]]の祭神'''八桙神'''と同一視する説がある。『特撰神名牒』、『大日本史神祇志』では祭神八桙神は建御名方神の子または曾孫としており<ref>延川和彦「諏訪氏系図」『修補諏訪氏系図.正編』飯田好太郎、大正10年、18頁。</ref><ref>宝賀寿男『長髄彦の後裔とその奉斎神社』2008年</ref>、『諸系譜』第六冊の「長公系図」には、弟を意味する「イロト（色止・伊呂止）」を名にする'''伊侶止命'''の子に'''八上乃命'''（やかみのみこと）があげられており、宝賀寿男は、これが八桙神にあたるとする説を提唱した<ref>宝賀寿男『長髄彦の後裔とその奉斎神社』2008年</ref>。
父祖の関係であるが、[[鈴木真年]]はその著『史略名称訓義』において「大和国[[城上郡]]登美ノ人、長髄モ同所ノ邑名、飛鳥[[事代主神]]ノ子」と記し、これは本荘氏の系譜『本荘家譜』において汙麻斯麻尼足尼命（宇摩志麻遅命）の補註に「母飛鳥大神之女、登美夜毘売」と記されていることからとされる。また[[太田亮]]も「磯城彦は即ち三輪氏の他ならず」と指摘している<ref>宝賀寿男「長髄彦と磯城県主の系譜」『三輪氏 大物主神の祭祀者』青垣出版、2015年</ref>。
[[宝賀寿男]]はその行動や世代関係などから、[[長髄彦]]を事代主神の子ではなくその弟と考え、[[建御名方神]]（[[諏訪氏]]祖）、[[天八現津彦命]]、観松彦色止命（[[長国造]]、[[都佐国造]]祖）と同一という説を唱えた。加えて顕著な龍蛇信仰{{要出典|date=2022年3月}}、本殿を持たない神社、[[銅鐸]]の分布、長野県に分布する「三輪」の地名と三輪信仰、互いに「ミワ」姓を名乗ることから三輪氏、諏訪氏が同族であると考え、また、長髄彦の子孫は二つの系統に別れたと考え、一方を[[出早雄命|伊豆速男命]]（[[片倉辺命]]）に始まる諏訪氏、もう一方を'''八杵命'''（八桙神）に始まる長国造、都佐国造とする説を提唱した<ref>宝賀寿男「長髄彦と磯城県主の系譜」『三輪氏 大物主神の祭祀者』青垣出版、2015年</ref>。
== 系譜 == [[建御名方神]]と[[八坂刀売神]]の子の一柱で、子に[[久留須神]]、[[比良夫神]]、[[若那売神]]、[[八立神]]（八玉神）、[[倉稲主神]]（[[倉稲魂神]]）がいる。
== 祀る神社 == * [[諏訪大社]]上社前宮 摂社 若御子社（[[長野県]][[茅野市]]宮川） * 諏訪大社下社春宮 摂社 若宮社（長野県[[諏訪郡]][[下諏訪町]]） * 諏訪大社下社秋宮 摂社 若宮社（長野県諏訪郡下諏訪町） * [[矢木神社]]（長野県諏訪郡下諏訪町）
== 参考文献 == {{Reflist}}
== 脚注 == {{Notelist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[諏訪氏]]
{{諏訪信仰}}
{{デフォルトソート:やきねのみこと}} [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:諏訪信仰|神やきねのみこと]]

revision.comment: More used with only one reference definition: 2 new references and 2 new reference calls.
revision.text:
{{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 八杵命 | 神祇 = [[国津神]] | 全名 = 八杵命 | 別名 = | 別称 = | 神階 = | 神格 = 開拓の神 | 陵所 = 伝承あり | 父 = [[建御名方神]] | 母 = [[八坂刀売神]] | 配偶者 = | 子 = [[久留須神]]、[[比良夫神]]、[[若那売神]]、[[八立神]]、[[倉稲主神]] | 神社 = [[諏訪大社]]、[[矢木神社]] 等 | 関連氏族 = [[小出氏 (禰宜大夫)|小出氏]] }}
'''八杵命'''（やきねのみこと、やぎのみこと）は、[[長野県]][[諏訪地方]]の民間伝承（[[諏訪信仰]]）の[[神 (神道)|神]]。
== 概要 == 史料としては『神氏系図』等に見える神で、[[建御名方神]]の子とされるが、[[御子神]]や子孫以外にこれといった記述はない。[[諏訪大社]]上社の'''五官祝'''（ごかんのほうり）の一つ、'''禰宜大夫'''（ねぎだゆう）を務めた'''[[小出氏 (禰宜大夫)|小出氏]]'''の祖神と伝えられるが、詳細な系図は現存しておらず、その全貌は不明である。
[[下諏訪町]]矢木に鎮座する矢木神社では[[信濃国]]開拓の神とされ、神陵が付近にあると伝わるが、詳細な位置は不明。
== 考証 == 八杵命は他の史書には見えず、同神を祀る神社も長野県以外に存在するか不明であるが、[[阿波国]]の[[式内社]]である[[八桙神社]]の祭神'''八桙神'''と同一視する説がある。『特撰神名牒』、『大日本史神祇志』では祭神八桙神は建御名方神の子または曾孫としており<ref>延川和彦「諏訪氏系図」『修補諏訪氏系図.正編』飯田好太郎、大正10年、18頁。</ref><ref name="#1">宝賀寿男『長髄彦の後裔とその奉斎神社』2008年</ref>、『諸系譜』第六冊の「長公系図」には、弟を意味する「イロト（色止・伊呂止）」を名にする'''伊侶止命'''の子に'''八上乃命'''（やかみのみこと）があげられており、宝賀寿男は、これが八桙神にあたるとする説を提唱した<ref name="#1"/>。
父祖の関係であるが、[[鈴木真年]]はその著『史略名称訓義』において「大和国[[城上郡]]登美ノ人、長髄モ同所ノ邑名、飛鳥[[事代主神]]ノ子」と記し、これは本荘氏の系譜『本荘家譜』において汙麻斯麻尼足尼命（宇摩志麻遅命）の補註に「母飛鳥大神之女、登美夜毘売」と記されていることからとされる。また[[太田亮]]も「磯城彦は即ち三輪氏の他ならず」と指摘している<ref name="#2">宝賀寿男「長髄彦と磯城県主の系譜」『三輪氏 大物主神の祭祀者』青垣出版、2015年</ref>。
[[宝賀寿男]]はその行動や世代関係などから、[[長髄彦]]を事代主神の子ではなくその弟と考え、[[建御名方神]]（[[諏訪氏]]祖）、[[天八現津彦命]]、観松彦色止命（[[長国造]]、[[都佐国造]]祖）と同一という説を唱えた。加えて顕著な龍蛇信仰{{要出典|date=2022年3月}}、本殿を持たない神社、[[銅鐸]]の分布、長野県に分布する「三輪」の地名と三輪信仰、互いに「ミワ」姓を名乗ることから三輪氏、諏訪氏が同族であると考え、また、長髄彦の子孫は二つの系統に別れたと考え、一方を[[出早雄命|伊豆速男命]]（[[片倉辺命]]）に始まる諏訪氏、もう一方を'''八杵命'''（八桙神）に始まる長国造、都佐国造とする説を提唱した<ref name="#2"/>。
== 系譜 == [[建御名方神]]と[[八坂刀売神]]の子の一柱で、子に[[久留須神]]、[[比良夫神]]、[[若那売神]]、[[八立神]]（八玉神）、[[倉稲主神]]（[[倉稲魂神]]）がいる。
== 祀る神社 == * [[諏訪大社]]上社前宮 摂社 若御子社（[[長野県]][[茅野市]]宮川） * 諏訪大社下社春宮 摂社 若宮社（長野県[[諏訪郡]][[下諏訪町]]） * 諏訪大社下社秋宮 摂社 若宮社（長野県諏訪郡下諏訪町） * [[矢木神社]]（長野県諏訪郡下諏訪町）

{{Multiple issues | Refimprove = December 2023 | Original research = December 2023 }} {{Infobox deity | name = Yakine no Mikoto | type = [[Kunitsukami|Kunitsu-kami]] | full_name = 八杵命 (Yakine no Mikoto) | other_names = | alternate_names = | rank = | divine_role = God of cultivation and development | burial_place = Traditions exist | father = [[Takeminakata-no-Kami]] | mother = [[Yasakatome-no-Kami]] | spouse = | children = [[Kurusu no Kami]], [[Hirahuo no Kami]], [[Wakaname no Kami]], [[Yatate no Kami]], [[Ōanamuchi|Kuraokami no Kami]] | shrines = [[Suwa Taisha]], [[Yagi Shrine]] | related_clans = [[Koide clan (Negi Daibu)|Koide clan]] }}
'''Yakine no Mikoto''' (八杵命, also read as Yaki no Mikoto) is a deity from the folk traditions of the [[Suwa region]] in [[Nagano Prefecture]], associated with the [[Suwa Faith]] (諏訪信仰). He is revered as a god of development and cultivation.
== Overview == Yakine no Mikoto is mentioned in historical sources such as the {{ill|Genealogy of the Deities|ja|神氏系図}}. He is described as a child of {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Kami|ja|建御名方神}}, but little is recorded beyond his lineage and descendants. The deity is regarded as the ancestral god of the {{ill|Koide clan (Negi Daibu)|ja|小出氏}}, who served as the '''Negi Daibu''' (禰宜大夫), one of the {{ill|Five Priestly Offices|ja|五官祝}} of [[Suwa Taisha]]'s Upper Shrine. However, no detailed genealogical records exist, leaving much of his story shrouded in mystery.
At [[Yagi Shrine]] in [[Shimosuwa, Nagano|Shimosuwa]], Yakine no Mikoto is venerated as a god of development in the historical [[Shinano Province]]. Local lore claims that his burial site is located nearby, but the exact location is unknown.
== Interpretations == Yakine no Mikoto does not appear in other historical texts, and it is uncertain whether shrines dedicated to him exist outside Nagano Prefecture. Some theories equate him with {{ill|Yahoko no Kami|ja|八桙神}}, the deity enshrined at [[Yahoko Shrine]], an ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} in [[Awa Province]].
== Lineage == Yakine no Mikoto is one of the children of {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Kami|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome-no-Kami|ja|八坂刀売神}}. His offspring include: * [[Kurusu no Kami]] * [[Hirahuo no Kami]] * [[Wakaname no Kami]] * [[Yatate no Kami]] (also known as Hachitama no Kami) * [[Ōanamuchi|Kuraokami no Kami]] (倉稲主神, also known as Kuraokami)
== Shrines == Yakine no Mikoto is venerated at the following locations: * [[Suwa Taisha]] Upper Shrine - {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine|ja|若御子社}} (Chino City, Nagano Prefecture) * Suwa Taisha Lower Shrine - {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine|ja|若宮社}} (Shimosuwa, Nagano Prefecture) * [[Yagi Shrine]] (Shimosuwa, Nagano Prefecture)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Notes == {{Notelist}}
== Related Articles == * [[Suwa Faith]] * [[Suwa clan]]

{{Multiple issues | Refimprove = December 2023 | Original research = December 2023 }} {{Infobox deity | name = Yakine no Mikoto | type = [[Kunitsukami|Kunitsu-kami]] | full_name = 八杵命 (Yakine no Mikoto) | other_names = | alternate_names = | rank = | divine_role = God of cultivation and development | burial_place = Traditions exist | father = [[Takeminakata-no-Kami]] | mother = [[Yasakatome-no-Kami]] | spouse = | children = [[Kurusu no Kami]], [[Hirahuo no Kami]], [[Wakaname no Kami]], [[Yatate no Kami]], [[Ōanamuchi|Kuraokami no Kami]] | shrines = [[Suwa Taisha]], [[Yagi Shrine]] | related_clans = [[Koide clan (Negi Daibu)|Koide clan]] }}

He is described as a child of {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Kami|ja|建御名方神}}, but little is recorded beyond his lineage and descendants. The deity is regarded as the ancestral god of the {{ill|Koide clan (Negi Daibu)|ja|小出氏}}, who served as the '''Negi Daibu''' (禰宜大夫), one of the {{ill|Five Priestly Offices|ja|五官祝}} of [[Suwa Taisha]]'s Upper Shrine. However, no detailed genealogical records exist, leaving much of his story shrouded in mystery.
At [[Yagi Shrine]] in [[Shimosuwa, Nagano|Shimosuwa]], Yakine no Mikoto is venerated as a god of development in the historical [[Shinano Province]]. Local lore claims that his burial site is located nearby, but the exact location is unknown.
== Interpretations == Yakine no Mikoto does not appear in other historical texts, and it is uncertain whether shrines dedicated to him exist outside Nagano Prefecture. Some theories equate him with {{ill|Yahoko no Kami|ja|八桙神}}, the deity enshrined at [[Yahoko Shrine]], an ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} in [[Awa Province]].
== Lineage == Yakine no Mikoto is one of the children of {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Kami|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome-no-Kami|ja|八坂刀売神}}. His offspring include: * [[Kurusu no Kami]] * [[Hirahuo no Kami]] * [[Wakaname no Kami]] * [[Yatate no Kami]] (also known as Hachitama no Kami) * [[Ōanamuchi|Kuraokami no Kami]] (倉稲主神, also known as Kuraokami)
== Shrines == Yakine no Mikoto is venerated at the following locations: * [[Suwa Taisha]] Upper Shrine - {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine|ja|若御子社}} (Chino City, Nagano Prefecture) * Suwa Taisha Lower Shrine - {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine|ja|若宮社}} (Shimosuwa, Nagano Prefecture) * [[Yagi Shrine]] (Shimosuwa, Nagano Prefecture)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Notes == {{Notelist}}
== Related Articles == * [[Suwa Faith]] * [[Suwa clan]]

revision.text:
{{Multiple issues | Refimprove = December 2023 | Original research = December 2023 }} {{Infobox deity | name = Yakine no Mikoto | type = [[Kunitsukami|Kunitsu-kami]] | full_name = 八杵命 (Yakine no Mikoto) | other_names = | alternate_names = | rank = | divine_role = God of cultivation and development | burial_place = Traditions exist | father = [[Takeminakata-no-Kami]] | mother = [[Yasakatome-no-Kami]] | spouse = | children = [[Kurusu no Kami]], [[Hirahuo no Kami]], [[Wakaname no Kami]], [[Yatate no Kami]], [[Ōanamuchi|Kuraokami no Kami]] | shrines = [[Suwa Taisha]], [[Yagi Shrine]] | related_clans = [[Koide clan (Negi Daibu)|Koide clan]] }}
'''Yakine no Mikoto''' (八杵命, also read as Yaki no Mikoto) is a deity from the folk traditions of the [[Suwa region]] in [[Nagano Prefecture]], associated with the [[Suwa Faith]] (諏訪信仰). He is revered as a god of development and cultivation.
== Overview == Yakine no Mikoto is mentioned in historical sources such as the {{ill|Genealogy of the Deities|ja|神氏系図}}. He is described as a child of {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Kami|ja|建御名方神}}, but little is recorded beyond his lineage and descendants. The deity is regarded as the ancestral god of the {{ill|Koide clan (Negi Daibu)|ja|小出氏}}, who served as the '''Negi Daibu''' (禰宜大夫), one of the {{ill|Five Priestly Offices|ja|五官祝}} of [[Suwa Taisha]]'s Upper Shrine. However, no detailed genealogical records exist, leaving much of his story shrouded in mystery.
At [[Yagi Shrine]] in [[Shimosuwa, Nagano|Shimosuwa]], Yakine no Mikoto is venerated as a god of development in the historical [[Shinano Province]]. Local lore claims that his burial site is located nearby, but the exact location is unknown.
== Interpretations == Yakine no Mikoto does not appear in other historical texts, and it is uncertain whether shrines dedicated to him exist outside Nagano Prefecture. Some theories equate him with {{ill|Yahoko no Kami|ja|八桙神}}, the deity enshrined at [[Yahoko Shrine]], an ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} in [[Awa Province]].
== Lineage == Yakine no Mikoto is one of the children of {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Kami|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome-no-Kami|ja|八坂刀売神}}. His offspring include: * [[Kurusu no Kami]] * [[Hirahuo no Kami]] * [[Wakaname no Kami]] * [[Yatate no Kami]] (also known as Hachitama no Kami) * [[Ōanamuchi|Kuraokami no Kami]] (倉稲主神, also known as Kuraokami)
== Shrines == Yakine no Mikoto is venerated at the following locations: * [[Suwa Taisha]] Upper Shrine - {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine|ja|若御子社}} (Chino City, Nagano Prefecture) * Suwa Taisha Lower Shrine - {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine|ja|若宮社}} (Shimosuwa, Nagano Prefecture) * [[Yagi Shrine]] (Shimosuwa, Nagano Prefecture)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Notes == {{Notelist}}
== Related Articles == * [[Suwa Faith]] * [[Suwa clan]]
{{Suwa Faith}} [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Shinto deities]] [[Category:Suwa Faith]] [[ja:八杵命]]

{{Multiple issues | Refimprove = December 2023 | Original research = December 2023 }} {{Infobox deity | name = Yakine no Mikoto | type = [[Kunitsukami|Kunitsu-kami]] | full_name = 八杵命 (Yakine no Mikoto) | other_names = | alternate_names = | rank = | divine_role = God of cultivation and development | burial_place = Traditions exist | father = [[Takeminakata-no-Kami]] | mother = [[Yasakatome-no-Kami]] | spouse = | children = [[Kurusu no Kami]], [[Hirahuo no Kami]], [[Wakaname no Kami]], [[Yatate no Kami]], [[Ōanamuchi|Kuraokami no Kami]] | shrines = [[Suwa Taisha]], [[Yagi Shrine]] | related_clans = [[Koide clan (Negi Daibu)|Koide clan]] }}
'''Yakine no Mikoto''' (八杵命, also read as Yaki no Mikoto) is a deity from the folk traditions of the [[Suwa region]] in [[Nagano Prefecture]], associated with the [[Suwa Faith]] (諏訪信仰). He is revered as a god of development and cultivation.
== Overview == Yakine no Mikoto is mentioned in historical sources such as the {{ill|Genealogy of the Deities|ja|神氏系図}}. He is described as a child of {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Kami|ja|建御名方神}}, but little is recorded beyond his lineage and descendants. The deity is regarded as the ancestral god of the {{ill|Koide clan (Negi Daibu)|ja|小出氏}}, who served as the '''Negi Daibu''' (禰宜大夫), one of the {{ill|Five Priestly Offices|ja|五官祝}} of [[Suwa Taisha]]'s Upper Shrine. However, no detailed genealogical records exist, leaving much of his story shrouded in mystery.
At [[Yagi Shrine]] in [[Shimosuwa, Nagano|Shimosuwa]], Yakine no Mikoto is venerated as a god of development in the historical [[Shinano Province]]. Local lore claims that his burial site is located nearby, but the exact location is unknown.
== Interpretations == Yakine no Mikoto does not appear in other historical texts, and it is uncertain whether shrines dedicated to him exist outside Nagano Prefecture. Some theories equate him with {{ill|Yahoko no Kami|ja|八桙神}}, the deity enshrined at [[Yahoko Shrine]], an ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} in [[Awa Province]].
== Lineage == Yakine no Mikoto is one of the children of {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Kami|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome-no-Kami|ja|八坂刀売神}}. His offspring include: * [[Kurusu no Kami]] * [[Hirahuo no Kami]] * [[Wakaname no Kami]] * [[Yatate no Kami]] (also known as Hachitama no Kami) * [[Ōanamuchi|Kuraokami no Kami]] (倉稲主神, also known as Kuraokami)
== Shrines == Yakine no Mikoto is venerated at the following locations: * [[Suwa Taisha]] Upper Shrine - {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine|ja|若御子社}} (Chino City, Nagano Prefecture) * Suwa Taisha Lower Shrine - {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine|ja|若宮社}} (Shimosuwa, Nagano Prefecture) * [[Yagi Shrine]] (Shimosuwa, Nagano Prefecture)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Notes == {{Notelist}}
== Related Articles == * [[Suwa Faith]] * [[Suwa clan]]
{{Suwa Faith}} [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Shinto deities]] [[Category:Suwa Faith]] [[ja:八杵命]]

{{Multiple issues | Refimprove = December 2023 | Original research = December 2023 }} {{Infobox deity | name = Yakine no Mikoto | type = [[Kunitsukami|Kunitsu-kami]] | full_name = 八杵命 (Yakine no Mikoto) | other_names = | alternate_names = | rank = | divine_role = God of cultivation and development | burial_place = Traditions exist | father = [[Takeminakata-no-Kami]] | mother = [[Yasakatome-no-Kami]] | spouse = | children = [[Kurusu no Kami]], [[Hirahuo no Kami]], [[Wakaname no Kami]], [[Yatate no Kami]], [[Ōanamuchi|Kuraokami no Kami]] | shrines = [[Suwa Taisha]], [[Yagi Shrine]] | related_clans = [[Koide clan (Negi Daibu)|Koide clan]] }}
'''Yakine no Mikoto''' (八杵命, also read as Yaki no Mikoto) is a deity from the folk traditions of the [[Suwa region]] in [[Nagano Prefecture]], associated with the [[Suwa Faith]] (諏訪信仰). He is revered as a god of development and cultivation.
== Overview == Yakine no Mikoto is mentioned in historical sources such as the {{ill|Genealogy of the Deities|ja|神氏系図}}. He is described as a child of {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Kami|ja|建御名方神}}, but little is recorded beyond his lineage and descendants. The deity is regarded as the ancestral god of the {{ill|Koide clan (Negi Daibu)|ja|小出氏}}, who served as the '''Negi Daibu''' (禰宜大夫), one of the {{ill|Five Priestly Offices|ja|五官祝}} of [[Suwa Taisha]]'s Upper Shrine. However, no detailed genealogical records exist, leaving much of his story shrouded in mystery.
At [[Yagi Shrine]] in [[Shimosuwa, Nagano|Shimosuwa]], Yakine no Mikoto is venerated as a god of development in the historical [[Shinano Province]]. Local lore claims that his burial site is located nearby, but the exact location is unknown.
== Interpretations == Yakine no Mikoto does not appear in other historical texts, and it is uncertain whether shrines dedicated to him exist outside Nagano Prefecture. Some theories equate him with {{ill|Yahoko no Kami|ja|八桙神}}, the deity enshrined at [[Yahoko Shrine]], an ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} in [[Awa Province]].
== Lineage == Yakine no Mikoto is one of the children of {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Kami|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome-no-Kami|ja|八坂刀売神}}. His offspring include: * [[Kurusu no Kami]] * [[Hirahuo no Kami]] * [[Wakaname no Kami]] * [[Yatate no Kami]] (also known as Hachitama no Kami) * [[Ōanamuchi|Kuraokami no Kami]] (倉稲主神, also known as Kuraokami)
== Shrines == Yakine no Mikoto is venerated at the following locations: * [[Suwa Taisha]] Upper Shrine - {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine|ja|若御子社}} (Chino City, Nagano Prefecture) * Suwa Taisha Lower Shrine - {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine|ja|若宮社}} (Shimosuwa, Nagano Prefecture) * [[Yagi Shrine]] (Shimosuwa, Nagano Prefecture)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Notes == {{Notelist}}
== Related Articles == * [[Suwa Faith]] * [[Suwa clan]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Shinto deities]] [[Category:Suwa Faith]] [[ja:八杵命]]

{{Multiple issues | Refimprove = December 2023 | Original research = December 2023 }} {{Infobox deity | name = Yakine no Mikoto | type = [[Kunitsukami|Kunitsu-kami]] | full_name = 八杵命 (Yakine no Mikoto) | other_names = | alternate_names = | rank = | divine_role = God of cultivation and development | burial_place = Traditions exist | father = [[Takeminakata-no-Kami]] | mother = [[Yasakatome-no-Kami]] | spouse = | children = [[Kurusu no Kami]], [[Hirahuo no Kami]], [[Wakaname no Kami]], [[Yatate no Kami]], [[Ōanamuchi|Kuraokami no Kami]] | shrines = [[Suwa Taisha]], [[Yagi Shrine]] | related_clans = [[Koide clan (Negi Daibu)|Koide clan]] }}
'''Yakine no Mikoto''' (八杵命, also read as Yaki no Mikoto) is a deity from the folk traditions of the [[Suwa region]] in [[Nagano Prefecture]], associated with the [[Suwa Faith]] (諏訪信仰). He is revered as a god of development and cultivation.
== Overview == Yakine no Mikoto is mentioned in historical sources such as the {{ill|Genealogy of the Deities|ja|神氏系図}}. He is described as a child of {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Kami|ja|建御名方神}}, but little is recorded beyond his lineage and descendants. The deity is regarded as the ancestral god of the {{ill|Koide clan (Negi Daibu)|ja|小出氏}}, who served as the '''Negi Daibu''' (禰宜大夫), one of the {{ill|Five Priestly Offices|ja|五官祝}} of [[Suwa Taisha]]'s Upper Shrine. However, no detailed genealogical records exist, leaving much of his story shrouded in mystery.
At [[Yagi Shrine]] in [[Shimosuwa, Nagano|Shimosuwa]], Yakine no Mikoto is venerated as a god of development in the historical [[Shinano Province]]. Local lore claims that his burial site is located nearby, but the exact location is unknown.
== Interpretations == Yakine no Mikoto does not appear in other historical texts, and it is uncertain whether shrines dedicated to him exist outside Nagano Prefecture. Some theories equate him with {{ill|Yahoko no Kami|ja|八桙神}}, the deity enshrined at [[Yahoko Shrine]], an ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} in [[Awa Province]].
== Lineage == Yakine no Mikoto is one of the children of {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Kami|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome-no-Kami|ja|八坂刀売神}}. His offspring include: * [[Kurusu no Kami]] * [[Hirahuo no Kami]] * [[Wakaname no Kami]] * [[Yatate no Kami]] (also known as Hachitama no Kami) * [[Ōanamuchi|Kuraokami no Kami]] (倉稲主神, also known as Kuraokami)
== Shrines == Yakine no Mikoto is venerated at the following locations: * [[Suwa Taisha]] Upper Shrine - {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine|ja|若御子社}} (Chino City, Nagano Prefecture) * Suwa Taisha Lower Shrine - {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine|ja|若宮社}} (Shimosuwa, Nagano Prefecture) * [[Yagi Shrine]] (Shimosuwa, Nagano Prefecture)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Notes == {{Notelist}}

'''Yakine no Mikoto''' (八杵命, also read as Yaki no Mikoto) is a deity from the folk traditions of the [[Suwa region]] in [[Nagano Prefecture]], associated with the [[Suwa Faith]] (諏訪信仰). He is revered as a god of development and cultivation.
== Overview == Yakine no Mikoto is mentioned in historical sources such as the {{ill|Genealogy of the Deities|ja|神氏系図}}. He is described as a child of {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Kami|ja|建御名方神}}, but little is recorded beyond his lineage and descendants. The deity is regarded as the ancestral god of the {{ill|Koide clan (Negi Daibu)|ja|小出氏}}, who served as the '''Negi Daibu''' (禰宜大夫), one of the {{ill|Five Priestly Offices|ja|五官祝}} of [[Suwa Taisha]]'s Upper Shrine. However, no detailed genealogical records exist, leaving much of his story shrouded in mystery.
At [[Yagi Shrine]] in [[Shimosuwa, Nagano|Shimosuwa]], Yakine no Mikoto is venerated as a god of development in the historical [[Shinano Province]]. Local lore claims that his burial site is located nearby, but the exact location is unknown.
== Interpretations == Yakine no Mikoto does not appear in other historical texts, and it is uncertain whether shrines dedicated to him exist outside Nagano Prefecture. Some theories equate him with {{ill|Yahoko no Kami|ja|八桙神}}, the deity enshrined at [[Yahoko Shrine]], an ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} in [[Awa Province]].
== Lineage == Yakine no Mikoto is one of the children of {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Kami|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome-no-Kami|ja|八坂刀売神}}. His offspring include: * [[Kurusu no Kami]] * [[Hirahuo no Kami]] * [[Wakaname no Kami]] * [[Yatate no Kami]] (also known as Hachitama no Kami) * [[Ōanamuchi|Kuraokami no Kami]] (倉稲主神, also known as Kuraokami)
== Shrines == Yakine no Mikoto is venerated at the following locations: * [[Suwa Taisha]] Upper Shrine - {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine|ja|若御子社}} (Chino City, Nagano Prefecture) * Suwa Taisha Lower Shrine - {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine|ja|若宮社}} (Shimosuwa, Nagano Prefecture) * [[Yagi Shrine]] (Shimosuwa, Nagano Prefecture)

{{Multiple issues | Refimprove = December 2023 | Original research = December 2023 }} {{Infobox deity | name = Yakine no Mikoto | type = [[Kunitsukami|Kunitsu-kami]] | full_name = 八杵命 (Yakine no Mikoto) | other_names = | alternate_names = | rank = | divine_role = God of cultivation and development | burial_place = Traditions exist | father = [[Takeminakata-no-Kami]] | mother = [[Yasakatome-no-Kami]] | spouse = | children = [[Kurusu no Kami]], [[Hirahuo no Kami]], [[Wakaname no Kami]], [[Yatate no Kami]], [[Ōanamuchi|Kuraokami no Kami]] | shrines = [[Suwa Taisha]], [[Yagi Shrine]] | related_clans = [[Koide clan (Negi Daibu)|Koide clan]] }}
'''Yakine no Mikoto''' (八杵命, also read as Yaki no Mikoto) is a deity from the folk traditions of the [[Suwa region]] in [[Nagano Prefecture]], associated with the [[Suwa Faith]] (諏訪信仰). He is revered as a god of development and cultivation.
== Overview == Yakine no Mikoto is mentioned in historical sources such as the {{ill|Genealogy of the Deities|ja|神氏系図}}. He is described as a child of {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Kami|ja|建御名方神}}, but little is recorded beyond his lineage and descendants. The deity is regarded as the ancestral god of the {{ill|Koide clan (Negi Daibu)|ja|小出氏}}, who served as the '''Negi Daibu''' (禰宜大夫), one of the {{ill|Five Priestly Offices|ja|五官祝}} of [[Suwa Taisha]]'s Upper Shrine. However, no detailed genealogical records exist, leaving much of his story shrouded in mystery.
At [[Yagi Shrine]] in [[Shimosuwa, Nagano|Shimosuwa]], Yakine no Mikoto is venerated as a god of development in the historical [[Shinano Province]]. Local lore claims that his burial site is located nearby, but the exact location is unknown.
== Interpretations == Yakine no Mikoto does not appear in other historical texts, and it is uncertain whether shrines dedicated to him exist outside Nagano Prefecture. Some theories equate him with {{ill|Yahoko no Kami|ja|八桙神}}, the deity enshrined at [[Yahoko Shrine]], an ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} in [[Awa Province]].
== Lineage == Yakine no Mikoto is one of the children of {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Kami|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome-no-Kami|ja|八坂刀売神}}. His offspring include: * [[Kurusu no Kami]] * [[Hirahuo no Kami]] * [[Wakaname no Kami]] * [[Yatate no Kami]] (also known as Hachitama no Kami) * [[Ōanamuchi|Kuraokami no Kami]] (倉稲主神, also known as Kuraokami)
== Shrines == Yakine no Mikoto is venerated at the following locations: * [[Suwa Taisha]] Upper Shrine - {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine|ja|若御子社}} (Chino City, Nagano Prefecture) * Suwa Taisha Lower Shrine - {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine|ja|若宮社}} (Shimosuwa, Nagano Prefecture) * [[Yagi Shrine]] (Shimosuwa, Nagano Prefecture)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Notes == {{Notelist}}
== Related Articles == * [[Suwa Faith]] * [[Suwa clan]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Shinto deities]] [[Category:Suwa Faith]] [[ja:八杵命]]

revision.text:
{{Multiple issues | Refimprove = December 2023 | Original research = December 2023 }} {{Infobox deity | name = Yakine no Mikoto | type = [[Kunitsukami|Kunitsu-kami]] | full_name = 八杵命 (Yakine no Mikoto) | other_names = | alternate_names = | rank = | divine_role = God of cultivation and development | burial_place = Traditions exist | father = [[Takeminakata-no-Kami]] | mother = [[Yasakatome-no-Kami]] | spouse = | children = [[Kurusu no Kami]], [[Hirahuo no Kami]], [[Wakaname no Kami]], [[Yatate no Kami]], [[Ōanamuchi|Kuraokami no Kami]] | shrines = [[Suwa Taisha]], [[Yagi Shrine]] | related_clans = [[Koide clan (Negi Daibu)|Koide clan]] }}
'''Yakine no Mikoto''' (八杵命, also read as Yaki no Mikoto) is a deity from the folk traditions of the [[Suwa region]] in [[Nagano Prefecture]], associated with the [[Suwa Faith]] (諏訪信仰). He is revered as a god of development and cultivation.
== Overview == Yakine no Mikoto is mentioned in historical sources such as the {{ill|Genealogy of the Deities|ja|神氏系図}}. He is described as a child of {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Kami|ja|建御名方神}}, but little is recorded beyond his lineage and descendants. The deity is regarded as the ancestral god of the {{ill|Koide clan (Negi Daibu)|ja|小出氏}}, who served as the '''Negi Daibu''' (禰宜大夫), one of the {{ill|Five Priestly Offices|ja|五官祝}} of [[Suwa Taisha]]'s Upper Shrine. However, no detailed genealogical records exist, leaving much of his story shrouded in mystery.
At [[Yagi Shrine]] in [[Shimosuwa, Nagano|Shimosuwa]], Yakine no Mikoto is venerated as a god of development in the historical [[Shinano Province]]. Local lore claims that his burial site is located nearby, but the exact location is unknown.
== Interpretations == Yakine no Mikoto does not appear in other historical texts, and it is uncertain whether shrines dedicated to him exist outside Nagano Prefecture. Some theories equate him with {{ill|Yahoko no Kami|ja|八桙神}}, the deity enshrined at [[Yahoko Shrine]], an ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} in [[Awa Province]].
== Lineage == Yakine no Mikoto is one of the children of {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Kami|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome-no-Kami|ja|八坂刀売神}}. His offspring include: * [[Kurusu no Kami]] * [[Hirahuo no Kami]] * [[Wakaname no Kami]] * [[Yatate no Kami]] (also known as Hachitama no Kami) * [[Ōanamuchi|Kuraokami no Kami]] (倉稲主神, also known as Kuraokami)
== Shrines == Yakine no Mikoto is venerated at the following locations: * [[Suwa Taisha]] Upper Shrine - {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine|ja|若御子社}} (Chino City, Nagano Prefecture) * Suwa Taisha Lower Shrine - {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine|ja|若宮社}} (Shimosuwa, Nagano Prefecture) * [[Yagi Shrine]] (Shimosuwa, Nagano Prefecture)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Notes == {{Notelist}}
== Related Articles == * [[Suwa Faith]] * [[Suwa clan]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Shinto deities]] [[Category:Suwa Faith]] [[ja:八杵命]]

{{Multiple issues | Refimprove = December 2023 | Original research = December 2023 }} {{Infobox deity | name = Yakine no Mikoto | type = [[Kunitsukami|Kunitsu-kami]] | full_name = 八杵命 (Yakine no Mikoto) | other_names = | alternate_names = | rank = | divine_role = God of cultivation and development | burial_place = Traditions exist | father = [[Takeminakata-no-Kami]] | mother = [[Yasakatome-no-Kami]] | spouse = | children = [[Kurusu no Kami]], [[Hirahuo no Kami]], [[Wakaname no Kami]], [[Yatate no Kami]], [[Ōanamuchi|Kuraokami no Kami]] | shrines = [[Suwa Taisha]], [[Yagi Shrine]] | related_clans = [[Koide clan (Negi Daibu)|Koide clan]] }}
'''Yakine no Mikoto''' (八杵命, also read as Yaki no Mikoto) is a deity from the folk traditions of the [[Suwa region]] in [[Nagano Prefecture]], associated with the [[Suwa Faith]] (諏訪信仰). He is revered as a god of development and cultivation.
== Overview == Yakine no Mikoto is mentioned in historical sources such as the {{ill|Genealogy of the Deities|ja|神氏系図}}. He is described as a child of {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Kami|ja|建御名方神}}, but little is recorded beyond his lineage and descendants. The deity is regarded as the ancestral god of the {{ill|Koide clan (Negi Daibu)|ja|小出氏}}, who served as the '''Negi Daibu''' (禰宜大夫), one of the {{ill|Five Priestly Offices|ja|五官祝}} of [[Suwa Taisha]]'s Upper Shrine. However, no detailed genealogical records exist, leaving much of his story shrouded in mystery.
At [[Yagi Shrine]] in [[Shimosuwa, Nagano|Shimosuwa]], Yakine no Mikoto is venerated as a god of development in the historical [[Shinano Province]]. Local lore claims that his burial site is located nearby, but the exact location is unknown.
== Interpretations == Yakine no Mikoto does not appear in other historical texts, and it is uncertain whether shrines dedicated to him exist outside Nagano Prefecture. Some theories equate him with {{ill|Yahoko no Kami|ja|八桙神}}, the deity enshrined at [[Yahoko Shrine]], an ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} in [[Awa Province]].
== Lineage == Yakine no Mikoto is one of the children of {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Kami|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome-no-Kami|ja|八坂刀売神}}. His offspring include: * [[Kurusu no Kami]] * [[Hirahuo no Kami]] * [[Wakaname no Kami]] * [[Yatate no Kami]] (also known as Hachitama no Kami) * [[Ōanamuchi|Kuraokami no Kami]] (倉稲主神, also known as Kuraokami)
== Shrines == Yakine no Mikoto is venerated at the following locations: * [[Suwa Taisha]] Upper Shrine - {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine|ja|若御子社}} (Chino City, Nagano Prefecture) * Suwa Taisha Lower Shrine - {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine|ja|若宮社}} (Shimosuwa, Nagano Prefecture) * [[Yagi Shrine]] (Shimosuwa, Nagano Prefecture)
== References == {{Reflist}}

revision.text:
{{Multiple issues | Refimprove = December 2023 | Original research = December 2023 }} {{Infobox deity | name = Yakine no Mikoto | type = [[Kunitsukami|Kunitsu-kami]] | full_name = 八杵命 (Yakine no Mikoto) | other_names = | alternate_names = | rank = | divine_role = God of cultivation and development | burial_place = Traditions exist | father = [[Takeminakata-no-Kami]] | mother = [[Yasakatome-no-Kami]] | spouse = | children = [[Kurusu no Kami]], [[Hirahuo no Kami]], [[Wakaname no Kami]], [[Yatate no Kami]], [[Ōanamuchi|Kuraokami no Kami]] | shrines = [[Suwa Taisha]], [[Yagi Shrine]] | related_clans = [[Koide clan (Negi Daibu)|Koide clan]] }}
'''Yakine no Mikoto''' (八杵命, also read as Yaki no Mikoto) is a deity from the folk traditions of the [[Suwa region]] in [[Nagano Prefecture]], associated with the [[Suwa Faith]] (諏訪信仰). He is revered as a god of development and cultivation.
== Overview == Yakine no Mikoto is mentioned in historical sources such as the {{ill|Genealogy of the Deities|ja|神氏系図}}. He is described as a child of {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Kami|ja|建御名方神}}, but little is recorded beyond his lineage and descendants. The deity is regarded as the ancestral god of the {{ill|Koide clan (Negi Daibu)|ja|小出氏}}, who served as the '''Negi Daibu''' (禰宜大夫), one of the {{ill|Five Priestly Offices|ja|五官祝}} of [[Suwa Taisha]]'s Upper Shrine. However, no detailed genealogical records exist, leaving much of his story shrouded in mystery.
At [[Yagi Shrine]] in [[Shimosuwa, Nagano|Shimosuwa]], Yakine no Mikoto is venerated as a god of development in the historical [[Shinano Province]]. Local lore claims that his burial site is located nearby, but the exact location is unknown.
== Interpretations == Yakine no Mikoto does not appear in other historical texts, and it is uncertain whether shrines dedicated to him exist outside Nagano Prefecture. Some theories equate him with {{ill|Yahoko no Kami|ja|八桙神}}, the deity enshrined at [[Yahoko Shrine]], an ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} in [[Awa Province]].
== Lineage == Yakine no Mikoto is one of the children of {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Kami|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome-no-Kami|ja|八坂刀売神}}. His offspring include: * [[Kurusu no Kami]] * [[Hirahuo no Kami]] * [[Wakaname no Kami]] * [[Yatate no Kami]] (also known as Hachitama no Kami) * [[Ōanamuchi|Kuraokami no Kami]] (倉稲主神, also known as Kuraokami)
== Shrines == Yakine no Mikoto is venerated at the following locations: * [[Suwa Taisha]] Upper Shrine - {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine|ja|若御子社}} (Chino City, Nagano Prefecture) * Suwa Taisha Lower Shrine - {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine|ja|若宮社}} (Shimosuwa, Nagano Prefecture) * [[Yagi Shrine]] (Shimosuwa, Nagano Prefecture)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Notes == {{Notelist}}
== Related Articles == * [[Suwa Faith]] * [[Suwa clan]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Shinto deities]] [[Category:Suwa Faith]] [[ja:八杵命]]

{{Multiple issues | Refimprove = December 2023 | Original research = December 2023 }} {{Infobox deity | name = Yakine no Mikoto | type = [[Kunitsukami|Kunitsukami]] | full_name = 八杵命 (Yakine no Mikoto) | other_names = | alternate_names = | rank = | divine_role = God of cultivation and development | burial_place = Traditions exist | father = [[Takeminakata-no-Kami]] | mother = [[Yasakatome-no-Kami]] | spouse = | children = [[Kurusu no Kami]], [[Hirahuo no Kami]], [[Wakaname no Kami]], [[Yatate no Kami]], [[Ōanamuchi|Kuraokami no Kami]] | shrines = [[Suwa Taisha]], [[Yagi Shrine]] | related_clans = [[Koide clan (Negi Daibu)|Koide clan]] }}
'''Yakine no Mikoto''' (八杵命, also read as Yaki no Mikoto) is a deity from the folk traditions of the [[Suwa region]] in [[Nagano Prefecture]], associated with the [[Suwa Faith]] (諏訪信仰). He is revered as a god of development and cultivation.
== Overview == Yakine no Mikoto is mentioned in historical sources such as the {{ill|Genealogy of the Deities|ja|神氏系図}}. He is described as a child of {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Kami|ja|建御名方神}}, but little is recorded beyond his lineage and descendants. The deity is regarded as the ancestral god of the {{ill|Koide clan (Negi Daibu)|ja|小出氏}}, who served as the '''Negi Daibu''' (禰宜大夫), one of the {{ill|Five Priestly Offices|ja|五官祝}} of [[Suwa Taisha]]'s Upper Shrine. However, no detailed genealogical records exist, leaving much of his story shrouded in mystery.
At [[Yagi Shrine]] in [[Shimosuwa, Nagano|Shimosuwa]], Yakine no Mikoto is venerated as a god of development in the historical [[Shinano Province]]. Local lore claims that his burial site is located nearby, but the exact location is unknown.
== Interpretations == Yakine no Mikoto does not appear in other historical texts, and it is uncertain whether shrines dedicated to him exist outside Nagano Prefecture. Some theories equate him with {{ill|Yahoko no Kami|ja|八桙神}}, the deity enshrined at [[Yahoko Shrine]], an ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} in [[Awa Province]].
== Lineage == Yakine no Mikoto is one of the children of {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Kami|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome-no-Kami|ja|八坂刀売神}}. His offspring include: * [[Kurusu no Kami]] * [[Hirahuo no Kami]] * [[Wakaname no Kami]] * [[Yatate no Kami]] (also known as Hachitama no Kami) * [[Ōanamuchi|Kuraokami no Kami]] (倉稲主神, also known as Kuraokami)
== Shrines == Yakine no Mikoto is venerated at the following locations: * [[Suwa Taisha]] Upper Shrine - {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine|ja|若御子社}} (Chino City, Nagano Prefecture) * Suwa Taisha Lower Shrine - {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine|ja|若宮社}} (Shimosuwa, Nagano Prefecture) * [[Yagi Shrine]] (Shimosuwa, Nagano Prefecture)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Notes == {{Notelist}}
== Related Articles == * [[Suwa Faith]] * [[Suwa clan]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Shinto deities]] [[Category:Suwa Faith]] [[ja:八杵命]]

{{Multiple issues | Refimprove = December 2023 | Original research = December 2023 }} {{Infobox deity | name = Yakine no Mikoto | type = [[Kunitsukami|Kunitsukami]] | full_name = 八杵命 (Yakine no Mikoto) | other_names = | alternate_names = | rank = | divine_role = God of cultivation and development | burial_place = Traditions exist | father = [[Takeminakata-no-Kami]] | mother = [[Yasakatome-no-Kami]] | spouse = | children = [[Kurusu no Kami]], [[Hirahuo no Kami]], [[Wakaname no Kami]], [[Yatate no Kami]], [[Ōanamuchi|Kuraokami no Kami]] | shrines = [[Suwa Taisha]], [[Yagi Shrine]] | related_clans = [[Koide clan (Negi Daibu)|Koide clan]] }}
'''Yakine no Mikoto''' (八杵命, also read as Yaki no Mikoto) is a deity from the folk traditions of the [[Suwa region]] in [[Nagano Prefecture]], associated with the [[Suwa Faith]] (諏訪信仰). He is revered as a god of development and cultivation.
== Overview == Yakine no Mikoto is mentioned in historical sources such as the {{ill|Genealogy of the Deities|ja|神氏系図}}. He is described as a child of {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Kami|ja|建御名方神}}, but little is recorded beyond his lineage and descendants. The deity is regarded as the ancestral god of the {{ill|Koide clan (Negi Daibu)|ja|小出氏}}, who served as the '''Negi Daibu''' (禰宜大夫), one of the {{ill|Five Priestly Offices|ja|五官祝}} of [[Suwa Taisha]]'s Upper Shrine. However, no detailed genealogical records exist, leaving much of his story shrouded in mystery.
At [[Yagi Shrine]] in [[Shimosuwa, Nagano|Shimosuwa]], Yakine no Mikoto is venerated as a god of development in the historical [[Shinano Province]]. Local lore claims that his burial site is located nearby, but the exact location is unknown.
== Interpretations == Yakine no Mikoto does not appear in other historical texts, and it is uncertain whether shrines dedicated to him exist outside Nagano Prefecture. Some theories equate him with {{ill|Yahoko no Kami|ja|八桙神}}, the deity enshrined at [[Yahoko Shrine]], an ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} in [[Awa Province]].
== Lineage == Yakine no Mikoto is one of the children of {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Kami|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome-no-Kami|ja|八坂刀売神}}. His offspring include: * [[Kurusu no Kami]] * [[Hirahuo no Kami]] * [[Wakaname no Kami]] * [[Yatate no Kami]] (also known as Hachitama no Kami) * [[Ōanamuchi|Kuraokami no Kami]] (倉稲主神, also known as Kuraokami)
== Shrines == Yakine no Mikoto is venerated at the following locations: * [[Suwa Taisha]] Upper Shrine - {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine|ja|若御子社}} (Chino City, Nagano Prefecture) * Suwa Taisha Lower Shrine - {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine|ja|若宮社}} (Shimosuwa, Nagano Prefecture) * [[Yagi Shrine]] (Shimosuwa, Nagano Prefecture)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Notes == {{Notelist}}
== Related Articles == * [[Suwa Faith]] * [[Suwa clan]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Shinto deities]] [[Category:Suwa Faith]] [[ja:八杵命]]

revision.text:
{{Multiple issues | Refimprove = December 2023 | Original research = December 2023 }} {{Infobox deity | name = Yakine no Mikoto | type = [[Kunitsukami|Kunitsukami]] | full_name = 八杵命 (Yakine no Mikoto) | other_names = | alternate_names = | rank = | divine_role = God of cultivation and development | burial_place = Traditions exist | father = [[Takeminakata-no-Kami]] | mother = [[Yasakatome-no-Kami]] | spouse = | children = [[Kurusu no Kami]], [[Hirahuo no Kami]], [[Wakaname no Kami]], [[Yatate no Kami]], [[Ōanamuchi|Kuraokami no Kami]] | shrines = [[Suwa Taisha]], [[Yagi Shrine]] | related_clans = [[Koide clan (Negi Daibu)|Koide clan]] }}
'''Yakine no Mikoto''' (八杵命, also read as Yaki no Mikoto) is a deity from the folk traditions of the [[Suwa region]] in [[Nagano Prefecture]], associated with the [[Suwa Faith]] (諏訪信仰). He is revered as a god of development and cultivation.
== Overview == Yakine no Mikoto is mentioned in historical sources such as the {{ill|Genealogy of the Deities|ja|神氏系図}}. He is described as a child of {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Kami|ja|建御名方神}}, but little is recorded beyond his lineage and descendants. The deity is regarded as the ancestral god of the {{ill|Koide clan (Negi Daibu)|ja|小出氏}}, who served as the '''Negi Daibu''' (禰宜大夫), one of the {{ill|Five Priestly Offices|ja|五官祝}} of [[Suwa Taisha]]'s Upper Shrine. However, no detailed genealogical records exist, leaving much of his story shrouded in mystery.
At [[Yagi Shrine]] in [[Shimosuwa, Nagano|Shimosuwa]], Yakine no Mikoto is venerated as a god of development in the historical [[Shinano Province]]. Local lore claims that his burial site is located nearby, but the exact location is unknown.
== Interpretations == Yakine no Mikoto does not appear in other historical texts, and it is uncertain whether shrines dedicated to him exist outside Nagano Prefecture. Some theories equate him with {{ill|Yahoko no Kami|ja|八桙神}}, the deity enshrined at [[Yahoko Shrine]], an ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} in [[Awa Province]].
== Lineage == Yakine no Mikoto is one of the children of {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Kami|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome-no-Kami|ja|八坂刀売神}}. His offspring include: * [[Kurusu no Kami]] * [[Hirahuo no Kami]] * [[Wakaname no Kami]] * [[Yatate no Kami]] (also known as Hachitama no Kami) * [[Ōanamuchi|Kuraokami no Kami]] (倉稲主神, also known as Kuraokami)
== Shrines == Yakine no Mikoto is venerated at the following locations: * [[Suwa Taisha]] Upper Shrine - {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine|ja|若御子社}} (Chino City, Nagano Prefecture) * Suwa Taisha Lower Shrine - {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine|ja|若宮社}} (Shimosuwa, Nagano Prefecture) * [[Yagi Shrine]] (Shimosuwa, Nagano Prefecture)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Notes == {{Notelist}}
== Related Articles == * [[Suwa Faith]] * [[Suwa clan]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Shinto deities]] [[Category:Suwa Faith]] [[ja:八杵命]]

{{Multiple issues | Refimprove = December 2023 | Original research = December 2023 }} {{Infobox deity | name = Yakine no Mikoto | type = [[Kunitsukami|Kunitsukami]] | full_name = 八杵命 (Yakine no Mikoto) | other_names = | alternate_names = | rank = | divine_role = God of cultivation and development | burial_place = Traditions exist | father = [[Takeminakata-no-Kami]] | mother = [[Yasakatome-no-Kami]] | spouse = | children = [[Kurusu no Kami]], [[Hirahuo no Kami]], [[Wakaname no Kami]], [[Yatate no Kami]], [[Ōanamuchi|Kuraokami no Kami]] | shrines = [[Suwa Taisha]], [[Yagi Shrine]] | related_clans = [[Koide clan (Negi Daibu)|Koide clan]] }}
'''Yakine no Mikoto''' (八杵命, also read as Yaki no Mikoto) is a deity from the folk traditions of the [[Suwa region]] in [[Nagano Prefecture]], associated with the [[Suwa Faith]] (諏訪信仰). He is revered as a god of development and cultivation.
== Overview == Yakine no Mikoto is mentioned in historical sources such as the {{ill|Genealogy of the Deities|ja|神氏系図}}. He is described as a child of {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Kami|ja|建御名方神}}, but little is recorded beyond his lineage and descendants. The deity is regarded as the ancestral god of the {{ill|Koide clan (Negi Daibu)|ja|小出氏}}, who served as the '''Negi Daibu''' (禰宜大夫), one of the {{ill|Five Priestly Offices|ja|五官祝}} of [[Suwa Taisha]]'s Upper Shrine. However, no detailed genealogical records exist, leaving much of his story shrouded in mystery.
At [[Yagi Shrine]] in [[Shimosuwa, Nagano|Shimosuwa]], Yakine no Mikoto is venerated as a god of development in the historical [[Shinano Province]]. Local lore claims that his burial site is located nearby, but the exact location is unknown.
== Interpretations == Yakine no Mikoto does not appear in other historical texts, and it is uncertain whether shrines dedicated to him exist outside Nagano Prefecture. Some theories equate him with {{ill|Yahoko no Kami|ja|八桙神}}, the deity enshrined at [[Yahoko Shrine]], an ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} in [[Awa Province]].
== Lineage == Yakine no Mikoto is one of the children of {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Kami|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome-no-Kami|ja|八坂刀売神}}. His offspring include: * [[Kurusu no Kami]] * [[Hirahuo no Kami]] * [[Wakaname no Kami]] * [[Yatate no Kami]] (also known as Hachitama no Kami) * [[Ōanamuchi|Kuraokami no Kami]] (倉稲主神, also known as Kuraokami)
== Shrines == Yakine no Mikoto is venerated at the following locations: * [[Suwa Taisha]] Upper Shrine - {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine|ja|若御子社}} (Chino City, Nagano Prefecture) * Suwa Taisha Lower Shrine - {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine|ja|若宮社}} (Shimosuwa, Nagano Prefecture) * [[Yagi Shrine]] (Shimosuwa, Nagano Prefecture)

{{Multiple issues | Refimprove = December 2023 | Original research = December 2023 }} {{Infobox deity | name = Yakine no Mikoto | type = [[Kunitsukami|Kunitsukami]] | full_name = 八杵命 (Yakine no Mikoto) | other_names = | alternate_names = | rank = | divine_role = God of cultivation and development | burial_place = Traditions exist | father = [[Takeminakata-no-Kami]] | mother = [[Yasakatome-no-Kami]] | spouse = | children = [[Kurusu no Kami]], [[Hirahuo no Kami]], [[Wakaname no Kami]], [[Yatate no Kami]], [[Ōanamuchi|Kuraokami no Kami]] | shrines = [[Suwa Taisha]], [[Yagi Shrine]] | related_clans = [[Koide clan (Negi Daibu)|Koide clan]] }}
'''Yakine no Mikoto''' (八杵命, also read as Yaki no Mikoto) is a deity from the folk traditions of the [[Suwa region]] in [[Nagano Prefecture]], associated with the [[Suwa Faith]] (諏訪信仰). He is revered as a god of development and cultivation.
== Overview == Yakine no Mikoto is mentioned in historical sources such as the {{ill|Genealogy of the Deities|ja|神氏系図}}. He is described as a child of {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Kami|ja|建御名方神}}, but little is recorded beyond his lineage and descendants. The deity is regarded as the ancestral god of the {{ill|Koide clan (Negi Daibu)|ja|小出氏}}, who served as the '''Negi Daibu''' (禰宜大夫), one of the {{ill|Five Priestly Offices|ja|五官祝}} of [[Suwa Taisha]]'s Upper Shrine. However, no detailed genealogical records exist, leaving much of his story shrouded in mystery.
At [[Yagi Shrine]] in [[Shimosuwa, Nagano|Shimosuwa]], Yakine no Mikoto is venerated as a god of development in the historical [[Shinano Province]]. Local lore claims that his burial site is located nearby, but the exact location is unknown.
== Interpretations == Yakine no Mikoto does not appear in other historical texts, and it is uncertain whether shrines dedicated to him exist outside Nagano Prefecture. Some theories equate him with {{ill|Yahoko no Kami|ja|八桙神}}, the deity enshrined at [[Yahoko Shrine]], an ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} in [[Awa Province]].
== Lineage == Yakine no Mikoto is one of the children of {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Kami|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome-no-Kami|ja|八坂刀売神}}. His offspring include: * [[Kurusu no Kami]] * [[Hirahuo no Kami]] * [[Wakaname no Kami]] * [[Yatate no Kami]] (also known as Hachitama no Kami) * [[Ōanamuchi|Kuraokami no Kami]] (倉稲主神, also known as Kuraokami)
== Shrines == Yakine no Mikoto is venerated at the following locations: * [[Suwa Taisha]] Upper Shrine - {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine|ja|若御子社}} (Chino City, Nagano Prefecture) * Suwa Taisha Lower Shrine - {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine|ja|若宮社}} (Shimosuwa, Nagano Prefecture) * [[Yagi Shrine]] (Shimosuwa, Nagano Prefecture)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Notes == {{Notelist}}
== Related Articles == * [[Suwa Faith]] * [[Suwa clan]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Shinto deities]] [[Category:Suwa Faith]] [[ja:八杵命]]

{{Multiple issues | Refimprove = December 2023 | Original research = December 2023 }} {{Infobox deity | name = Yakine no Mikoto | type = [[Kunitsukami|Kunitsukami]] | full_name = 八杵命 (Yakine no Mikoto) | other_names = | alternate_names = | rank = | divine_role = God of cultivation and development | burial_place = Traditions exist | father = [[Takeminakata-no-Kami]] | mother = [[Yasakatome-no-Kami]] | spouse = | children = [[Kurusu no Kami]], [[Hirahuo no Kami]], [[Wakaname no Kami]], [[Yatate no Kami]], [[Ōanamuchi|Kuraokami no Kami]] | shrines = [[Suwa Taisha]], [[Yagi Shrine]] | related_clans = [[Koide clan (Negi Daibu)|Koide clan]] }}
'''Yakine no Mikoto''' (八杵命, also read as Yaki no Mikoto) is a deity from the folk traditions of the [[Suwa region]] in [[Nagano Prefecture]], associated with the [[Suwa Faith]] (諏訪信仰). He is revered as a god of development and cultivation.
== Overview == Yakine no Mikoto is mentioned in historical sources such as the {{ill|Genealogy of the Deities|ja|神氏系図}}. He is described as a child of {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Kami|ja|建御名方神}}, but little is recorded beyond his lineage and descendants. The deity is regarded as the ancestral god of the {{ill|Koide clan (Negi Daibu)|ja|小出氏}}, who served as the '''Negi Daibu''' (禰宜大夫), one of the {{ill|Five Priestly Offices|ja|五官祝}} of [[Suwa Taisha]]'s Upper Shrine. However, no detailed genealogical records exist, leaving much of his story shrouded in mystery.
At [[Yagi Shrine]] in [[Shimosuwa, Nagano|Shimosuwa]], Yakine no Mikoto is venerated as a god of development in the historical [[Shinano Province]]. Local lore claims that his burial site is located nearby, but the exact location is unknown.
== Interpretations == Yakine no Mikoto does not appear in other historical texts, and it is uncertain whether shrines dedicated to him exist outside Nagano Prefecture. Some theories equate him with {{ill|Yahoko no Kami|ja|八桙神}}, the deity enshrined at [[Yahoko Shrine]], an ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} in [[Awa Province]].
== Lineage == Yakine no Mikoto is one of the children of {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Kami|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome-no-Kami|ja|八坂刀売神}}. His offspring include: * [[Kurusu no Kami]] * [[Hirahuo no Kami]] * [[Wakaname no Kami]] * [[Yatate no Kami]] (also known as Hachitama no Kami) * [[Ōanamuchi|Kuraokami no Kami]] (倉稲主神, also known as Kuraokami)
== Shrines == Yakine no Mikoto is venerated at the following locations: * [[Suwa Taisha]] Upper Shrine - {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine|ja|若御子社}} (Chino City, Nagano Prefecture) * Suwa Taisha Lower Shrine - {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine|ja|若宮社}} (Shimosuwa, Nagano Prefecture) * [[Yagi Shrine]] (Shimosuwa, Nagano Prefecture)
== References == {{Reflist}}

{{Multiple issues | Refimprove = December 2023 | Original research = December 2023 }} {{Infobox deity | name = Yakine no Mikoto | type = [[Kunitsukami|Kunitsukami]] | full_name = 八杵命 (Yakine no Mikoto) | other_names = | alternate_names = | rank = | divine_role = God of cultivation and development | burial_place = Traditions exist | father = [[Takeminakata-no-Kami]] | mother = [[Yasakatome-no-Kami]] | spouse = | children = [[Kurusu no Kami]], [[Hirahuo no Kami]], [[Wakaname no Kami]], [[Yatate no Kami]], [[Ōanamuchi|Kuraokami no Kami]] | shrines = [[Suwa Taisha]], [[Yagi Shrine]] | related_clans = [[Koide clan (Negi Daibu)|Koide clan]] }}
'''Yakine no Mikoto''' (八杵命, also read as Yaki no Mikoto) is a deity from the folk traditions of the [[Suwa region]] in [[Nagano Prefecture]], associated with the [[Suwa Faith]] (諏訪信仰). He is revered as a god of development and cultivation.
== Overview == Yakine no Mikoto is mentioned in historical sources such as the {{ill|Genealogy of the Deities|ja|神氏系図}}. He is described as a child of {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Kami|ja|建御名方神}}, but little is recorded beyond his lineage and descendants. The deity is regarded as the ancestral god of the {{ill|Koide clan (Negi Daibu)|ja|小出氏}}, who served as the '''Negi Daibu''' (禰宜大夫), one of the {{ill|Five Priestly Offices|ja|五官祝}} of [[Suwa Taisha]]'s Upper Shrine. However, no detailed genealogical records exist, leaving much of his story shrouded in mystery.
At [[Yagi Shrine]] in [[Shimosuwa, Nagano|Shimosuwa]], Yakine no Mikoto is venerated as a god of development in the historical [[Shinano Province]]. Local lore claims that his burial site is located nearby, but the exact location is unknown.
== Interpretations == Yakine no Mikoto does not appear in other historical texts, and it is uncertain whether shrines dedicated to him exist outside Nagano Prefecture. Some theories equate him with {{ill|Yahoko no Kami|ja|八桙神}}, the deity enshrined at [[Yahoko Shrine]], an ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} in [[Awa Province]].
== Lineage == Yakine no Mikoto is one of the children of {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Kami|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome-no-Kami|ja|八坂刀売神}}. His offspring include: * [[Kurusu no Kami]] * [[Hirahuo no Kami]] * [[Wakaname no Kami]] * [[Yatate no Kami]] (also known as Hachitama no Kami) * [[Ōanamuchi|Kuraokami no Kami]] (倉稲主神, also known as Kuraokami)
== Shrines == Yakine no Mikoto is venerated at the following locations: * [[Suwa Taisha]] Upper Shrine - {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine|ja|若御子社}} (Chino City, Nagano Prefecture) * Suwa Taisha Lower Shrine - {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine|ja|若宮社}} (Shimosuwa, Nagano Prefecture) * [[Yagi Shrine]] (Shimosuwa, Nagano Prefecture)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:八杵命]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q65249081]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Multiple issues | Refimprove = December 2023 | Original research = December 2023 }} {{Infobox deity | name = Yakine no Mikoto | type = [[Kunitsukami|Kunitsukami]] | full_name = 八杵命 (Yakine no Mikoto) | other_names = | alternate_names = | Rank = | divine_role = God of cultivation and development | burial_place = Traditions exist | father = [[Takeminakata-no-Kami]] | mother = [[Yasakatome|Yasakatome-no-Kami]] | spouse = | children = [[Kurusu no Kami]], [[Hirahuo no Kami]], [[Wakaname no Kami]], [[Yatate no Kami]], [[:en:Ōanamuchi|Kuraokami no Kami]] | shrines = [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]], [[Yagi Shrine]] | related_clans = [[Koide clan (Negi Daibu)|Koide clan]] }}
'''Yakine no Mikoto''' (八杵命, also read as Yaki no Mikoto) is a deity from the folk traditions of the [[Suwa Region|Suwa region]] in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], associated with the [[Suwa Faith]] (諏訪信仰). He is revered as a god of development and cultivation.
== Overview == Yakine no Mikoto is mentioned in historical sources such as the {{ill|Genealogy of the Deities|ja|神氏系図|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genealogy of the Deities|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. He is described as a child of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], but little is recorded beyond his lineage and descendants. The deity is regarded as the ancestral god of the {{ill|Koide clan (Negi Daibu)|ja|小出氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koide clan (Negi Daibu)|qq=}}, who served as the '''Negi Daibu''' (禰宜大夫), one of the {{ill|Five Priestly Offices|ja|五官祝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Priestly Offices|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]]'s Upper Shrine. However, no detailed genealogical records exist, leaving much of his story shrouded in mystery.
At [[Yagi Shrine]] in [[:en:Shimosuwa, Nagano|Shimosuwa]], Yakine no Mikoto is venerated as a god of development in the historical [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]]. Local lore claims that his burial site is located nearby, but the exact location is unknown.
== Interpretations == Yakine no Mikoto does not appear in other historical texts, and it is uncertain whether shrines dedicated to him exist outside Nagano Prefecture. Some theories equate him with {{ill|Yahoko no Kami|ja|八桙神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahoko no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the deity enshrined at [[Yahoko Shrine]], an ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} in [[Awa Province]].
== Lineage == Yakine no Mikoto is one of the children of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome-no-Kami]]. His offspring include: * [[Kurusu no Kami]] * [[Hirahuo no Kami]] * [[Wakaname no Kami]] * [[Yatate no Kami]] (also known as Hachitama no Kami) * [[:en:Ōanamuchi|Kuraokami no Kami]] (倉稲主神, also known as Kuraokami)
== Shrines == Yakine no Mikoto is venerated at the following locations: * [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]] Upper Shrine - {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine|ja|若御子社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Chino City, Nagano Prefecture) * Suwa Taisha Lower Shrine - {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine|ja|若宮社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Shrine|qq=}} (Shimosuwa, Nagano Prefecture) * [[Yagi Shrine]] (Shimosuwa, Nagano Prefecture)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Notes == {{Notelist}}
== Related Articles == * [[Suwa Faith]] * [[Suwa clan]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}}

'''Yakine no Mikoto''' (八杵命, also read as Yaki no Mikoto) is a deity from the folk traditions of the [[Suwa Region|Suwa region]] in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], associated with the [[Suwa Faith]] (諏訪信仰). He is revered as a god of development and cultivation.
== Overview == Yakine no Mikoto is mentioned in historical sources such as the {{ill|Genealogy of the Deities|ja|神氏系図|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genealogy of the Deities|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. He is described as a child of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], but little is recorded beyond his lineage and descendants. The deity is regarded as the ancestral god of the {{ill|Koide clan (Negi Daibu)|ja|小出氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koide clan (Negi Daibu)|qq=}}, who served as the '''Negi Daibu''' (禰宜大夫), one of the {{ill|Five Priestly Offices|ja|五官祝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Priestly Offices|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]]'s Upper Shrine. However, no detailed genealogical records exist, leaving much of his story shrouded in mystery.
At [[Yagi Shrine]] in [[:en:Shimosuwa, Nagano|Shimosuwa]], Yakine no Mikoto is venerated as a god of development in the historical [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]]. Local lore claims that his burial site is located nearby, but the exact location is unknown.
== Interpretations == Yakine no Mikoto does not appear in other historical texts, and it is uncertain whether shrines dedicated to him exist outside Nagano Prefecture. Some theories equate him with {{ill|Yahoko no Kami|ja|八桙神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahoko no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the deity enshrined at [[Yahoko Shrine]], an ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} in [[Awa Province]].
== Lineage == Yakine no Mikoto is one of the children of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome-no-Kami]]. His offspring include: * [[Kurusu no Kami]] * [[Hirahuo no Kami]] * [[Wakaname no Kami]] * [[Yatate no Kami]] (also known as Hachitama no Kami) * [[:en:Ōanamuchi|Kuraokami no Kami]] (倉稲主神, also known as Kuraokami)
== Shrines == Yakine no Mikoto is venerated at the following locations: * [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]] Upper Shrine - {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine|ja|若御子社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Chino City, Nagano Prefecture) * Suwa Taisha Lower Shrine - {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine|ja|若宮社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Shrine|qq=}} (Shimosuwa, Nagano Prefecture) * [[Yagi Shrine]] (Shimosuwa, Nagano Prefecture)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Notes == {{Notelist}}
== Related Articles == * [[Suwa Faith]] * [[Suwa clan]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}}

'''Yakine no Mikoto''' (八杵命, also read as Yaki no Mikoto) is a deity from the folk traditions of the [[Suwa Region|Suwa region]] in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], associated with the [[Suwa Faith]] (諏訪信仰). He is revered as a god of development and cultivation.
== Overview == Yakine no Mikoto is mentioned in historical sources such as the {{ill|Genealogy of the Deities|ja|神氏系図|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genealogy of the Deities|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. He is described as a child of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], but little is recorded beyond his lineage and descendants. The deity is regarded as the ancestral god of the {{ill|Koide clan (Negi Daibu)|ja|小出氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koide clan (Negi Daibu)|qq=}}, who served as the '''Negi Daibu''' (禰宜大夫), one of the {{ill|Five Priestly Offices|ja|五官祝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Priestly Offices|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]]'s Upper Shrine. However, no detailed genealogical records exist, leaving much of his story shrouded in mystery.
At [[Yagi Shrine]] in [[:en:Shimosuwa, Nagano|Shimosuwa]], Yakine no Mikoto is venerated as a god of development in the historical [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]]. Local lore claims that his burial site is located nearby, but the exact location is unknown.
== Interpretations == Yakine no Mikoto does not appear in other historical texts, and it is uncertain whether shrines dedicated to him exist outside Nagano Prefecture. Some theories equate him with {{ill|Yahoko no Kami|ja|八桙神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahoko no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the deity enshrined at [[Yahoko Shrine]], an ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} in [[Awa Province]].
== Lineage == Yakine no Mikoto is one of the children of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome-no-Kami]]. His offspring include: * [[Kurusu no Kami]] * [[Hirahuo no Kami]] * [[Wakaname no Kami]] * [[Yatate no Kami]] (also known as Hachitama no Kami) * [[:en:Ōanamuchi|Kuraokami no Kami]] (倉稲主神, also known as Kuraokami)
== Shrines == Yakine no Mikoto is venerated at the following locations: * [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]] Upper Shrine - {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine|ja|若御子社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Chino City, Nagano Prefecture) * Suwa Taisha Lower Shrine - {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine|ja|若宮社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Shrine|qq=}} (Shimosuwa, Nagano Prefecture) * [[Yagi Shrine]] (Shimosuwa, Nagano Prefecture)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:八杵命]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q65249081]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Multiple issues | Refimprove = December 2023 | Original research = December 2023 }} {{Infobox deity | name = Yakine no Mikoto | type = [[Kunitsukami|Kunitsukami]] | full_name = 八杵命 (Yakine no Mikoto) | other_names = | alternate_names = | Rank = | divine_role = God of cultivation and development | burial_place = Traditions exist | father = [[Takeminakata-no-Kami]] | mother = [[Yasakatome|Yasakatome-no-Kami]] | spouse = | children = [[Kurusu no Kami]], [[Hirahuo no Kami]], [[Wakaname no Kami]], [[Yatate no Kami]], [[:en:Ōanamuchi|Kuraokami no Kami]] | shrines = [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]], [[Yagi Shrine]] | related_clans = [[Koide clan (Negi Daibu)|Koide clan]] }}
'''Yakine no Mikoto''' (八杵命, also read as Yaki no Mikoto) is a deity from the folk traditions of the [[Suwa Region|Suwa region]] in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], associated with the [[Suwa Faith]] (諏訪信仰). He is revered as a god of development and cultivation.
== Overview == Yakine no Mikoto is mentioned in historical sources such as the {{ill|Genealogy of the Deities|ja|神氏系図|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genealogy of the Deities|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. He is described as a child of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], but little is recorded beyond his lineage and descendants. The deity is regarded as the ancestral god of the {{ill|Koide clan (Negi Daibu)|ja|小出氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koide clan (Negi Daibu)|qq=}}, who served as the '''Negi Daibu''' (禰宜大夫), one of the {{ill|Five Priestly Offices|ja|五官祝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Priestly Offices|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]]'s Upper Shrine. However, no detailed genealogical records exist, leaving much of his story shrouded in mystery.
At [[Yagi Shrine]] in [[:en:Shimosuwa, Nagano|Shimosuwa]], Yakine no Mikoto is venerated as a god of development in the historical [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]]. Local lore claims that his burial site is located nearby, but the exact location is unknown.
== Interpretations == Yakine no Mikoto does not appear in other historical texts, and it is uncertain whether shrines dedicated to him exist outside Nagano Prefecture. Some theories equate him with {{ill|Yahoko no Kami|ja|八桙神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahoko no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the deity enshrined at [[Yahoko Shrine]], an ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} in [[Awa Province]].
== Lineage == Yakine no Mikoto is one of the children of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome-no-Kami]]. His offspring include: * [[Kurusu no Kami]] * [[Hirahuo no Kami]] * [[Wakaname no Kami]] * [[Yatate no Kami]] (also known as Hachitama no Kami) * [[:en:Ōanamuchi|Kuraokami no Kami]] (倉稲主神, also known as Kuraokami)
== Shrines == Yakine no Mikoto is venerated at the following locations: * [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]] Upper Shrine - {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine|ja|若御子社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Chino City, Nagano Prefecture) * Suwa Taisha Lower Shrine - {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine|ja|若宮社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Shrine|qq=}} (Shimosuwa, Nagano Prefecture) * [[Yagi Shrine]] (Shimosuwa, Nagano Prefecture)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Notes == {{Notelist}}
== Related Articles == * [[Suwa Faith]] * [[Suwa clan]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}}

'''Yakine no Mikoto''' (八杵命, also read as Yaki no Mikoto) is a deity from the folk traditions of the [[Suwa Region|Suwa region]] in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], associated with the [[Suwa Faith]] (諏訪信仰). He is revered as a god of development and cultivation.

He is described as a child of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], but little is recorded beyond his lineage and descendants. The deity is regarded as the ancestral god of the {{ill|Koide clan (Negi Daibu)|ja|小出氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koide clan (Negi Daibu)|qq=}}, who served as the '''Negi Daibu''' (禰宜大夫), one of the {{ill|Five Priestly Offices|ja|五官祝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Priestly Offices|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]]'s Upper Shrine. '''Yakine no Mikoto''' (八杵命, also read as Yaki no Mikoto) is a deity from the folk traditions of the [[Suwa Region|Suwa region]] in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], associated with the [[Suwa Faith]] (諏訪信仰). He is revered as a god of development and cultivation.
== Overview == Yakine no Mikoto is mentioned in historical sources such as the {{ill|Genealogy of the Deities|ja|神氏系図|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genealogy of the Deities|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. He is described as a child of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], but little is recorded beyond his lineage and descendants. The deity is regarded as the ancestral god of the {{ill|Koide clan (Negi Daibu)|ja|小出氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koide clan (Negi Daibu)|qq=}}, who served as the '''Negi Daibu''' (禰宜大夫), one of the {{ill|Five Priestly Offices|ja|五官祝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Priestly Offices|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]]'s Upper Shrine. However, no detailed genealogical records exist, leaving much of his story shrouded in mystery.
At [[Yagi Shrine]] in [[:en:Shimosuwa, Nagano|Shimosuwa]], Yakine no Mikoto is venerated as a god of development in the historical [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]]. Local lore claims that his burial site is located nearby, but the exact location is unknown.
== Interpretations == Yakine no Mikoto does not appear in other historical texts, and it is uncertain whether shrines dedicated to him exist outside Nagano Prefecture. Some theories equate him with {{ill|Yahoko no Kami|ja|八桙神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahoko no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the deity enshrined at [[Yahoko Shrine]], an ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} in [[Awa Province]].
== Lineage == Yakine no Mikoto is one of the children of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome-no-Kami]]. His offspring include: * [[Kurusu no Kami]] * [[Hirahuo no Kami]] * [[Wakaname no Kami]] * [[Yatate no Kami]] (also known as Hachitama no Kami) * [[:en:Ōanamuchi|Kuraokami no Kami]] (倉稲主神, also known as Kuraokami)

'''Yakine no Mikoto''' (八杵命, also read as Yaki no Mikoto) is a deity from the folk traditions of the [[Suwa Region|Suwa region]] in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}}, associated with the [[Suwa Faith]] (諏訪信仰). He is revered as a god of development and cultivation.
== Overview == Yakine no Mikoto is mentioned in historical sources such as the {{ill|Genealogy of the Deities|ja|神氏系図|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genealogy of the Deities|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. He is described as a child of {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata}}, but little is recorded beyond his lineage and descendants. The deity is regarded as the ancestral god of the {{ill|Koide clan (Negi Daibu)|ja|小出氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koide clan (Negi Daibu)|qq=}}, who served as the '''Negi Daibu''' (禰宜大夫), one of the {{ill|Five Priestly Offices|ja|五官祝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Priestly Offices|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]]'s Upper Shrine. However, no detailed genealogical records exist, leaving much of his story shrouded in mystery.
At [[Yagi Shrine]] in [[:en:Shimosuwa, Nagano|Shimosuwa]], Yakine no Mikoto is venerated as a god of development in the historical {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province}}. Local lore claims that his burial site is located nearby, but the exact location is unknown.
== Interpretations == Yakine no Mikoto does not appear in other historical texts, and it is uncertain whether shrines dedicated to him exist outside Nagano Prefecture. Some theories equate him with {{ill|Yahoko no Kami|ja|八桙神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahoko no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the deity enshrined at [[Yahoko Shrine]], an ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} in [[Awa Province]].
== Lineage == Yakine no Mikoto is one of the children of {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|en|Yasakatome|WD=Q9150392|lt=Yasakatome-no-Kami}}. His offspring include: * [[Kurusu no Kami]] * [[Hirahuo no Kami]] * [[Wakaname no Kami]] * [[Yatate no Kami]] (also known as Hachitama no Kami) * [[:en:Ōanamuchi|Kuraokami no Kami]] (倉稲主神, also known as Kuraokami)
== Shrines == Yakine no Mikoto is venerated at the following locations: * [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]] Upper Shrine - {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine|ja|若御子社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Chino City, Nagano Prefecture) * Suwa Taisha Lower Shrine - {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine|ja|若宮社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Shrine|qq=}} (Shimosuwa, Nagano Prefecture) * [[Yagi Shrine]] (Shimosuwa, Nagano Prefecture)
